title: Optional columns
summary: Learn how to insert data into tables with optional columns, using NULL and default values in SQL.

sketchBlocks.title: What are optional columns?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, some columns in a table can be optional, meaning you don't have to provide a value for them when inserting a new row. These columns either allow NULL values or have a DEFAULT value defined. For example, in the `inventory_items` table, the `notes` column is optional because it allows NULL, and the `status`, `is_test`, and `last_updated` columns have default values.

```sql
CREATE TABLE inventory_items (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  category TEXT NOT NULL,
  price REAL NOT NULL,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
  is_test INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  notes TEXT NULL,
  last_updated TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '2026-02-01'
);
```

sketchBlocks.title: Inserting with optional columns
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When inserting data, you can skip optional columns in your column list. SQL will use the DEFAULT value if defined, or NULL if the column allows it. For example, to insert a new item without specifying `notes` or `status`:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price) VALUES (7, 'Desk Organizer', 'Accessories', 8.50);
```
This will set `status` to 'draft', `is_test` to 0, `notes` to NULL, and `last_updated` to '2026-02-01' for the new row.

sketchBlocks.title: Explicit NULL and DEFAULT values
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
You can also explicitly set a column to NULL (if allowed) or to its default value using the DEFAULT keyword:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, notes) VALUES (8, 'Sample Keychain', 'Accessories', 3.00, NULL);
```
Or, to use the default for `status`:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status) VALUES (9, 'Trial Cup', 'Kitchen', 6.00, DEFAULT);
```

quizDraft.title: Insert a row with only required columns
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new item into `inventory_items` with id 10, name 'Mini Poster', category 'Decor', and price 5.00. Do not specify any optional columns. Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: List only the required columns in the INSERT statement. Optional columns will use their defaults or NULL.

help.concept: When inserting a row, you can omit columns that have defaults or allow NULL. SQL will fill in those columns automatically.
help.hint_1: Use only id, name, category, and price in your column list.
help.hint_2: The omitted columns will be set to their default values or NULL as defined in the table schema.

quizDraft.solutionCode: INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price) VALUES (10, 'Mini Poster', 'Decor', 5.00);

quizDraft.title: Insert with explicit NULL for an optional column
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new item into `inventory_items` with id 11, name 'Test Magnet', category 'Accessories', price 2.50, and notes set to NULL. All other columns should use their defaults. Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: Include the notes column and set its value to NULL. Omit other optional columns.

help.concept: You can explicitly set a column to NULL if it allows NULL values. Other optional columns can be omitted to use their defaults.
help.hint_1: Add notes to your column list and set its value to NULL.
help.hint_2: Don't specify status, is_test, or last_updated; they will use their default values.

quizDraft.solutionCode: INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, notes) VALUES (11, 'Test Magnet', 'Accessories', 2.50, NULL);

quizDraft.title: Insert using DEFAULT keyword
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new item into `inventory_items` with id 12, name 'Sample Bottle', category 'Kitchen', price 12.00, and status set to its default value using the DEFAULT keyword. All other optional columns should use their defaults. Write the SQL statement.
quizDraft.hint: Include status in your column list and use the DEFAULT keyword for its value.

help.concept: The DEFAULT keyword lets you explicitly set a column to its default value in an INSERT statement.
help.hint_1: Add status to your column list and use DEFAULT as its value.
help.hint_2: Omit other optional columns to use their defaults or NULL.

quizDraft.solutionCode: INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status) VALUES (12, 'Sample Bottle', 'Kitchen', 12.00, DEFAULT);

quizDraft.title: Which columns are optional in inventory_items?
quizDraft.prompt: Select all columns in the `inventory_items` table that are optional when inserting a new row (can be omitted from the column list).
quizDraft.hint: Look for columns that allow NULL or have a DEFAULT value.

help.concept: A column is optional if it allows NULL or has a DEFAULT value. NOT NULL columns without defaults are required.
help.hint_1: Check the table definition for DEFAULT values and NULL allowances.
help.hint_2: Columns like notes (NULL) and status (DEFAULT) are optional.

quizDraft.title: Default value for status
quizDraft.prompt: What is the default value for the `status` column in `inventory_items`?
quizDraft.hint: Check the table definition for the DEFAULT assigned to status.

help.concept: A DEFAULT value is used when no value is provided for a column during insertion.
help.hint_1: Look for the DEFAULT keyword in the CREATE TABLE statement for status.
help.hint_2: The default is a string value set in the schema.

quizDraft.template: The default value for status is [blank].
